bias_defense_notes: Draft positions were all 0 (LLM prior). Shuffled deterministically using seed. Final distribution: pos0=1 (20%), pos1=2 (40%), pos2=1 (20%), pos3=1 (20%). max_share=0.40 — constraint satisfied (<=0.40). All 4 positions used.

questions.objective_ref: LO1 — Explain what it means for a function to have one job, and why that matters for testing.
questions.stem_summary: Student claims mixing compute + file-write in one function is fine; asks why that reasoning is wrong.

questions.objective_ref: LO2 — Identify a function doing more than one job by reading its body and name.
questions.stem_summary: Given function name validate_and_save_mark, what does the name alone reveal?

questions.objective_ref: LO3 — Use the 'and' naming heuristic to detect multi-job functions.
questions.stem_summary: Which of four function names most clearly signals a single-job function?

questions.objective_ref: LO4 — Refactor a multi-job function into two or more single-job functions.
questions.stem_summary: After splitting compute_and_save_average into two functions, how do you connect them at the call site?

questions.objective_ref: LO5 — Write a short spec-first manual test for a single-job function.
questions.stem_summary: Which approach correctly describes a manual test for compute_average?
